QualityReason: Every-page local book fixture for long-form text, headings, running headers, vector rules, and book-layout PDF display confidence. The PDF is ignored by git and populated locally from test-pdfs/manifests/local-real-world-books.json.
Notes: Focused all-pages scan logs/render-quality/local-real-world-books.json verified 454/455 pages as raw PASS. Page 36 formerly carried a lower-quality text-rasterization exception (#584); #710 root-caused it to platform (CoreText) glyph-mask coverage in DrawText and fixed it with outline-path text fills - the full-book rescan (logs/render-quality/book-after-710.json) is 455/455 raw PASS with page 36 matching all required references. #652 investigated a further page-36 symptom (em-dash visually colliding with the preceding word) hypothesized to be a CFF FontMatrix bug; that hypothesis was refuted at the byte level (Top DICT has no FontMatrix operator in either font resource involved, both share a byte-identical embedded CFF program). The actual cause: RenderText's embedded/byte-to-glyph glyph-ID draw path (SkiaRenderer.Text.cs) dispatched glyph runs via SKTextBlobBuilder.AddRun with Skia's default (hmtx-only) positioning, which has no notion of PDF Tc/Tw (character/word spacing) — those were only applied to the *tracked cursor* used for the next Tj/Tf's position, not to the actual on-canvas glyph layout. With Tw=-0.588 over 10 spaces in the "Supporters of Free Software maintain that the goals of their movement" run, the drawn glyphs (unaware of Tw) landed ~6pt further right than the Tw-adjusted cursor, so "movement"'s trailing "t" visually merged with the em-dash that correctly started at the Tw-adjusted position. Fixed by explicitly positioning glyphs (AddPositionedRun) using the same /Widths+Tc+Tw cumulative formula already used by the non-embedded fallback path, whenever Tc or Tw is non-zero. Page 36 mutool-diff (DifferentialMetrics.Compare, 150 DPI): 9.55% -> 0.59% differing pixels.

Target.Reason: Focused all-pages local book scan verified these pages as raw PASS against the available reference set.

1-35.QualityReason: Long-form local reading PDF page raw-passes against the reference renderer set.

Target.Reason: Raw PASS against the full reference set after #710: fill text is rasterized from the glyph outline path (Skia analytic scan converter) instead of platform (CoreText) glyph masks, removing the ~+0.45px-per-stem weight inflation on this embedded Type 1C page. Focused all-pages rescan logs/render-quality/book-after-710.json: 455/455 raw PASS, page 36 MATCHES_ALL_REQUIRED (mutool diff 9.55% -> 4.1%; pdftocairo diff 1.4%).

36.QualityReason: Formerly the #584 'embedded Type 1C text renders darker/heavier' page (EMBEDDED_CFF_TEXT_RASTERIZATION_DARKER_THAN_REFERENCE_CONSENSUS, PASS_ONE vs pdftocairo). Root-caused in #710 as platform glyph-mask coverage, not CFF handling and not Skia scan conversion; fixed by outline-path text fills, gated by TextRasterInkParityTests.
